src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Migrate Spring Security HTTP configuration from the deprecated fluent API (using .and()) to the new lambda-based DSL required by Spring Security 6.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The .and() method and the old fluent builder pattern for HttpSecurity were removed in Spring Security 6; code using them will fail to compile.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Update the application of the authorization server configurer to use the new .with() method and withDefaults() static import.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The .apply() method for applying configurers was removed in Spring Security 6; it must be replaced with .with() to compile.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add the static import for withDefaults() required by the new Spring Security 6 configuration style.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The withDefaults() method is used in the new configuration syntax but is not imported by default; missing import causes a compile error.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Migrate HttpSecurity configuration from the deprecated fluent API (using .and()) to the lambda-based DSL required by Spring Security 6.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The .and() method and the old fluent API for HttpSecurity were removed in Spring Security 6, causing compilation errors if not migrated to the lambda-based configuration style.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Annotate serialVersionUID with @Serial to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: Starting with Java 14, serializable fields like serialVersionUID must be explicitly annotated with @Serial to avoid compiler warnings or errors depending on the strictness of the build configuration (e.g., -Werror).

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 17+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: Java 17+ enforces that serialVersionUID fields must be annotated with @Serial or the code will fail to compile with an error about missing annotation.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Import java.io.Serial to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is part of java.io package in Java 17+; without the import, the code will fail to compile.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: Compilation error: 'serialVersionUID' is not an implicitly declared member of a serializable class (requires @Serial annotation since Java 14).

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization best practices and Java 21 strictness.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: Java 21 (and modern linters/compilers) may flag or error on serialVersionUID fields lacking the @Serial annotation, as it is the standard way to mark serialization metadata since Java 14.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.why_exists: The code will fail to compile if the @Serial annotation is used without importing java.io.Serial.
